title: The 11 Best Herbal Creams for Shingles Pain Relief

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids, commonly known as milk thistle, are good for shingles because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce the pain and discomfort caused by the virus, allowing the body to heal faster.
Quercetin is good for shingles because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain, itching, and swelling associated with the shingles rash.
Saponins are good for shingles because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the pain and swelling associated with the shingles rash.

Tannins, also known as milk thistle, are good for shingles because they have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that may help reduce pain, itching, and blistering associated with the shingles virus.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis cream can be helpful in treating shingles. Shingles is a skin condition caused by the varicella-zoster virus, which also causes chickenpox. It causes a painful rash and blisters on the skin. The cream is made from the flowers of the Calendula officinalis plant, which has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. This means it can help reduce the redness and swelling of the blisters, and prevent infection by killing off bacteria that can get into the open sores. Calendula officinalis cream is also known to promote healing and soothe the skin, making it a potential relief for the pain and discomfort associated with shingles. Some people find it helps reduce the itching and burning sensations that come with shingles. Applying the cream to the affected skin may also help prevent scarring and promote a faster recovery.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Silybum marianum herbal cream for shingles, first you need to prepare the Silybum marianum plant. This plant, also known as milk thistle, contains compounds that help reduce inflammation and pain. Dry the plant leaves and flowers by tying them in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry, dark place. Once dry, grind the plant material into a fine powder. Next, mix 1 part of the Silybum marianum powder with 2 parts of a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a clean glass jar. Heat the mixture gently in a double boiler or a saucepan with a heat-proof bowl on top until the oil is warm and the powder is fully dissolved. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids and transfer the liquid to a clean jar. Add a few drops of a natural preservative, such as vitamin E oil, to the cream to prevent it from spoiling. Finally, store the Silybum marianum herbal cream in the refrigerator and apply it to the affected area 2-3 times a day to help soothe shingles symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for shingles

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anthraquinones are good for shingles because they have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that help reduce the severity and duration of shingles by soothing the skin, reducing pain, and preventing the virus from multiplying.

Aloe-emodin has been shown to be effective in treating shingles due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce pain and itching caused by the virus, and its ability to boost the immune system, allowing the body to fight off the shingles virus more efficiently.
Aloin is good for shingles because it has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can help reduce pain, inflammation, and healing time of shingles lesions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis cream is a natural remedy that can help soothe and calm shingles. Shingles is a painful skin condition caused by the varicella-zoster virus. The virus causes small, itchy blisters to appear on the skin, which can be very uncomfortable. Aloe barbadensis cream works by reducing inflammation and itching in the affected area. This helps to provide relief and comfort to people suffering from shingles. The cream is made from the gel of the aloe vera plant, which has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. These properties help to speed up the healing process and prevent infection. Aloe barbadensis cream is also gentle and non-irritating, making it suitable for sensitive skin. When applied to the skin, it creates a protective barrier that helps to lock in moisture and soothe the skin. This makes it an effective remedy for shingles, helping to reduce the discomfort and pain associated with the condition.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Silybum marianum herbal creams that help with shingles, you will need dried Silybum marianum flowers, a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, beeswax, a double boiler, and a glass jar. First, mix 1 cup of dried Silybum marianum flowers with 2 cups of carrier oil in a heat-proof bowl. Then, place the bowl over a double boiler and heat the mixture over low heat for about 2 hours, stirring occasionally, until the oil turns greenish in color and has a strong herbal scent. Next, strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another bowl, discarding the solids. In a separate saucepan, melt 2 tablespoons of beeswax over low heat, then add the infused oil mixture to the melted beeswax and stir until well combined. Remove the mixture from the heat and pour it into a glass jar. Allow the cream to cool and solidify before using it on affected areas to help soothe and reduce the pain of shingles.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for shingles

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerols are good for shingles because they have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that help reduce pain and fight the virus that causes shingles.
Shogaols are good for shingles because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and swelling associated with the shingles rash.

Polyphenols are good for shingles because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce pain, itching, and the severity of shingles by fighting off the virus and promoting healing.
Zingerone is good for shingles because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the pain and swelling caused by the shingles virus.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, has been used for centuries to help with pain and inflammation. Shingles is a painful condition caused by the herpes zoster virus, which can cause a rash and severe pain on one side of the body. The creams made from Zingiber officinale can help to reduce the pain and inflammation associated with shingles. The anti-inflammatory compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to reduce swelling and ease pain. When applied topically, these creams can provide quick relief from the discomfort and itching associated with shingles. Additionally, ginger has antioxidant properties that can help to reduce the risk of complications from shingles, such as postherpetic neuralgia, a condition where the pain from shingles persists even after the rash has healed.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Silybum marianum herbal creams that help with shingles, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You will need dried Silybum marianum flowers, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a preservative. First, infuse the dried flowers in the carrier oil by placing them in a clean glass jar and letting it sit in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking the jar daily. This process extracts the active compounds from the flowers into the oil. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Next, melt the beeswax in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in short increments, stirring between each heating. Once the beeswax is fully melted, slowly add the infused oil to the melted wax, stirring constantly. The mixture will thicken and turn into a creamy texture. Finally, add a preservative to prevent the growth of bacteria and mold, and pour the cream into clean containers. Let the cream cool and harden before use. Apply the cream to the affected area 2-3 times a day to help soothe and heal the shingles rash.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for shingles

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Catechins are good for shingles because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce the severity and duration of the shingles rash, promoting healing and relief from pain and discomfort.
Terpinen-4-ol is good for shingles because it has strong antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and discomfort associated with the condition.
Quercetin is good for shingles because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling associated with the shingles rash.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil, is a natural ingredient found in many creams. It has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the pain and discomfort of shingles. Shingles is a viral infection that causes a painful rash. The cream can soothe the skin and reduce the severity of the rash. Tea tree oil can also help prevent infection, which can be a complication of shingles.  When applied topically, the cream can help to reduce the itching and burning sensations associated with shingles. The anti-inflammatory properties of tea tree oil can also help to reduce swelling and redness. This can make it easier for the skin to heal and recover from the infection. Furthermore, the cream may help to speed up the recovery process by promoting the growth of healthy skin cells. The soothing and calming effects of the cream can also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can be a significant factor in the healing process.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Silybum marianum herbal creams that help with shingles, you will need to start by harvesting the seeds of the Silybum marianum plant, also known as milk thistle. Make sure to identify the plant correctly and harvest the seeds when they are ripe and dry. Then, dry the seeds further by spreading them out in a single layer on a paper bag or a clean cloth in a warm, dry place. Once the seeds are completely dry, grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or a mortar and pestle. Next, mix the powdered seeds with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a ratio of about 1:5, and heat the mixture gently over low heat in a double boiler until the oil is warm and the seeds are fully incorporated. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool, then strain it through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove any remaining seeds or sediment. Finally, add a thickening agent such as beeswax or shea butter to the oil mixture and stir well until it is fully incorporated, then pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and harden before use. Apply the cream to the affected area 2-3 times a day to help alleviate symptoms of shingles.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for shingles

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Alkylamides in <built-in method capitalize of str object at 0x76c3f92c68b0> Silybum marianum have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce pain, itching, and inflammation caused by shingles, making it a potential natural remedy for the condition.

Iridoids are good for shingles because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling associated with the condition.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea cream can be helpful for shingles. This is because the cream contains plant chemicals that have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can reduce the swelling and pain associated with shingles. Shingles is a viral infection that causes a painful rash. The rash can be very itchy and uncomfortable. The anti-inflammatory properties in Echinacea purpurea cream can help soothe the rash and reduce the discomfort. Some people also find that Echinacea purpurea cream can help speed up the healing process of the rash. The cream may be applied directly to the affected area, allowing the plant chemicals to penetrate the skin. This can be especially helpful if you're experiencing pain or discomfort from the shingles rash.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Silybum marianum herbal creams that help with shingles, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. Silybum marianum, also known as milk thistle, is a key ingredient in treating shingles due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. First, dry the Silybum marianum leaves and flowers completely to prevent spoilage. Then, mix 2 tablespoons of dried Silybum marianum with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil or olive oil in a small bowl. Heat the oil mixture in a double boiler or a saucepan with low heat for about 10 minutes, allowing the Silybum marianum to infuse into the oil. Let the mixture cool and then strain it using a cheesecloth or a fine mesh to remove the Silybum marianum solids. Add a few drops of a carrier oil like jojoba or sweet almond oil to the infused oil to create a smooth, spreadable cream. Transfer the mixture to a small container and store it in the refrigerator. Apply the cream directly to the shingles rash 2-3 times a day to help reduce pain, inflammation, and itching.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for shingles

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Hyperforin has been shown to be beneficial for shingles because it has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the pain and discomfort associated with the condition.

Quercetin is good for shingles because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the pain and swelling associated with shingles by blocking the production of chemical messengers that cause inflammation.
Kaempferol is good for shingles because it has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that may help reduce pain, itching, and blistering associated with the shingles virus.
Tannins have been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the pain, itching, and swelling associated with shingles, a condition caused by the varicella-zoster virus.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum cream, also known as St. John's Wort, is a popular natural remedy for shingles. Shingles is a painful rash caused by the herpes zoster virus, which can leave scars and long-term nerve damage. Hypericum perforatum cream has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which help to reduce the severity and duration of shingles symptoms. The cream can be applied directly to the affected skin to ease the pain, itching, and burning sensations associated with shingles. It is also believed to help speed up the healing process by promoting the growth of new skin cells and reducing scarring. Many people find that using Hypericum perforatum cream alongside medical treatment, such as antiviral medication, helps to manage their symptoms more effectively. By reducing inflammation and killing the virus, Hypericum perforatum cream can help to alleviate the discomfort and promote recovery from shingles.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Silybum marianum herbal cream to help with shingles, you'll need a few simple ingredients and some basic equipment. First, dry the Silybum marianum flowers and leaves in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying them. Then, combine 2 tablespoons of the dried Silybum marianum with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Heat the mixture until the beeswax melts and the coconut oil and beeswax are fully incorporated. Remove the bowl from the heat and let it cool slightly before stirring in 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel and 2 drops of vitamin E oil. Pour the mixture into small containers or jars and let it cool completely before use. Once the cream is cool and solid, apply it to the affected area 2-3 times a day to help soothe and heal shingles symptoms. Note: Always do a patch test before using any new herbal cream, and consult with a healthcare professional before using it to treat a medical condition.

Aloe-emodin is good for shingles because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and inflammation caused by the shingles virus, and also has antiviral properties that can help fight the virus itself.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera cream can help with shingles because it has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can reduce swelling and redness on the skin. Shingles cause painful blisters that can be itchy and uncomfortable. The cooling effect of aloe vera cream can help calm the skin and bring down the temperature of the blisters.  Aloe vera cream also has antibacterial properties, which can help prevent infections in the blisters. Shingles can leave scars, but applying aloe vera cream regularly can promote healing and reduce the risk of scarring. Additionally, aloe vera cream can moisturize the skin and reduce dryness, which can be a problem after shingles.  Many people find aloe vera cream easy to apply and gentle on the skin. It's often used with other treatments, like pain relief medications, to help manage shingles symptoms. Some people also use it to help with the itching and discomfort of shingles.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Silybum marianum herbal cream that helps with shingles, you will need dried Silybum marianum plant, a carrier oil such as coconut oil or olive oil, beeswax, and a few simple kitchen tools. First, grind the dried Silybum marianum plant into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, mix the powder with a carrier oil in a small saucepan, heating the mixture over low heat while stirring constantly until the powder is fully dissolved. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool slightly. Then, add a small amount of melted beeswax to the mixture, stirring until the wax is fully incorporated. Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar or tin and let it cool and solidify completely. Once solid, your Silybum marianum herbal cream is ready to use. Apply a small amount to the affected area 2-3 times a day, gently massaging it in to help soothe and calm the skin.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Capsaicin is good for shingles because it has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that can help reduce the severe nerve pain associated with shingles.
Quercetin in Silybum Marianum is good for shingles because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the pain and swelling associated with shingles.

Polyphenols have been found to be beneficial in reducing the severity and duration of shingles, likely due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the nerve pain and skin rash associated with the condition.

remedies.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum cream is used to help with shingles pain. Shingles is a viral rash that causes burning pain, itching, and numbness. The Capsicum annuum cream contains a chemical called capsaicin, which is the same stuff that makes chili peppers hot. Capsaicin works by blocking the pain signals to the brain, so you don't feel as much pain. It also reduces inflammation and swelling, which can make the shingles rash look smaller and less red. Many people find that applying Capsicum annuum cream to the affected area helps to ease their shingles pain and makes them feel better.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Silybum marianum herbal creams that help with shingles, you will need to start by harvesting or purchasing the dried milk thistle seeds, which are the part of the plant used for medicinal purposes. Next, dry the seeds further in a low-temperature oven (around 150°F) for 2-3 hours to ensure they are completely dry. Then, crush the seeds into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle. Combine the powdered seeds with a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a ratio of about 1 part seeds to 10 parts oil. For example, mix 1 tablespoon of powdered seeds with 10 tablespoons of oil. Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water, stirring occasionally, until the seeds have infused into the oil. This process can take anywhere from 30 minutes to several hours, depending on the temperature and the strength of infusion desired. Once the infusion is complete, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar. Allow the infused oil to cool before mixing it with a cream base, such as shea butter or beeswax, to create a rich and nourishing cream that can be applied directly to the skin to help alleviate shingles symptoms.

Shingles is a painful rash caused by the varicella-zoster virus, which can leave painful blisters and sores on the skin. Arnica montana cream contains a compound called sesquiterpene lactones, which has anti-inflammatory properties. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Silybum marianum herbal cream for shingles, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: dried Silybum marianum leaves, coconut oil, shea butter, beeswax, and essential oils like tea tree oil and lavender oil. First, dry the Silybum marianum leaves in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying them for several weeks. Once dry, grind the leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or blender. Next, melt the coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in short intervals. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Silybum marianum herbal creams that help with shingles, you will need to gather the necessary ingredients and tools. First, dry Silybum marianum flowers and leaves in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying them. Then, mix one part of the dried herbs with two parts of a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a clean glass jar. intro_desc: Using herbal creams can be a helpful way to soothe and heal shingles. One herb that can be beneficial is Calendula officinalis, also known as marigold. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling, making it easier to manage shingles symptoms. Calendula can also help protect the skin from further irritation and promote healing.  Another herb that can be useful is Aloe barbadensis, also known as aloe vera. It is known for its cooling and soothing properties, which can help alleviate pain and itching associated with shingles. Aloe vera can also help lock in moisture, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of scarring.  Shingles can be a painful and uncomfortable condition that affects many people. If left untreated or managed incorrectly, it can lead to more severe symptoms and complications. Fixing the issue of shingles is essential to improve the quality of life. By using herbal creams with herbs like Calendula and Aloe vera, people can find relief from symptoms and promote faster healing.  When shingles symptoms are properly managed, people can feel more comfortable and confident in their daily activities. They can engage in hobbies and social activities without feeling held back by pain and discomfort. By taking steps to manage shingles, people can improve their overall well-being and quality of life. This can lead to a greater sense of independence and freedom to live life as they choose.
supplementary_best: A combination of Aloe vera, Calendula officinalis, and Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams can provide relief from shingles symptoms. Aloe vera cream helps soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Calendula officinalis cream, rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, aids in reducing pain and discomfort associated with shingles. Melaleuca alternifolia cream contains tea tree oil, known for its antiviral and antibacterial properties, which can help combat the underlying herpes zoster virus that causes shingles. This combination addresses multiple aspects of shingles relief: skin comfort, pain reduction, and viral suppression.
supplementary_avoid: When dealing with shingles, it's crucial to avoid certain herbal creams that may worsen the condition or interact with medications. One such herb is 'Ginkgo biloba', which can thin the blood and increase the risk of bleeding. This is a concern for people with shingles, as they may be taking antiviral medications or pain relievers that also thin the blood. Using a Ginkgo biloba cream can increase the risk of bleeding, especially if the shingles rash is open or if the person has other bleeding disorders.  Another herb to avoid is 'Valeriana officinalis', also known as valerian root. While it's often used to promote relaxation and sleep, valerian root can interact with some antiviral medications, which may reduce their effectiveness. This can make it harder for the body to fight off the shingles virus.  'Urtica dioica', or stinging nettle, can also be problematic. Although it's often used to reduce inflammation and itching, stinging nettle can cause an allergic reaction in some people, which may worsen the shingles rash. This can lead to increased redness, swelling, and discomfort.  'Euphorbia pilosa' and 'Euphorbia lathyris' should also be avoided. While they have anti-inflammatory properties, they can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. This can be particularly concerning for people with shingles, as the skin is already sensitive and fragile. Using a cream containing these herbs can lead to further irritation and discomfort.  It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal creams when dealing with shingles. They can provide guidance on safe and effective options to manage symptoms and promote healing.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aloe vera gel can help soothe the skin and reduce itching and redness caused by shingles. When applied topically, it can also help calm the inflammation and promote healing of the skin. Apple cider vinegar has antiviral properties that may help reduce the severity of shingles symptoms, but more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Oatmeal baths can provide relief from itching and discomfort, and can be especially helpful in reducing irritation and inflammation in sensitive areas. Epsom salt baths can also help reduce inflammation, ease pain, and promote relaxation, which is essential for recovering from shingles. Adding essential oils such as tea tree oil or lavender oil to bath water may also help reduce stress and promote healing. The warmth and moisture of Epsom salt baths can also help loosen and remove crusty skin, promoting faster healing.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have shingles may also experience other health issues that can be helped with medicinal herbs. For example, shingles can cause nerve pain, which can be relieved with capsaicin, a compound found in chili peppers. Capsaicin works by blocking the pain signals to the brain.  Some people with shingles may develop postherpetic neuralgia (PHN), a condition characterized by persistent nerve pain. The herb St. John's Wort has been shown to help with PHN by reducing inflammation and pain. Its active compounds, hyperforin and hypericin, have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.  Shingles can also cause stress and anxiety due to the emotional and physical discomfort. The herb passionflower has been used to treat anxiety and insomnia. Its active compounds, flavonoids and alkaloids, have a calming effect on the nervous system.  Additionally, people with shingles may experience fatigue, which can be alleviated with the herb ashwagandha. Ashwagandha is an adaptogen that helps the body adapt to stress and promotes energy and vitality.  The herb turmeric also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help with shingles-related inflammation and pain. Turmeric contains curcumin, a compound that blocks the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.  It's essential to note that while medicinal herbs can help alleviate symptoms, they should not be used as a replacement for medical treatment. Consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially if you're already taking medication or have underlying health conditions.
